steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the perimeter formula for a square
steps.content:
The perimeter of a square is the sum of all four sides. Since all sides of a square are equal, the formula is:
$$P = 4s$$where $s$ is the side length.

steps.title: Substitute the given side length and calculate
steps.content:
Substitute $s = 45$ into the formula:
$$P = 4(45) = 180$$
The perimeter is 180.
